title: Kerala Budget: Budget announces industry benefits to tourism sector
content: According to the Budget, necessary legal amendments will be made to make more land available for tourism in the plantation sector

title: Kerala Budget: Planning to be abandoned, says Binoy Viswam
content: Revised Budget backtracks from welfare policies followed by the previous Left Democratic Front government, he says

title: No provision for Kerala to withdraw from PM SHRI deal: Chief Minister V. D. Satheesan
content: Chief Minister says only Union government had the right to withdraw from the PM SHRI agreement after giving a notice of one month. There was no provision in the agreement to keep the scheme implementation in abeyance either

title: SC says plea seeking CBI probe into May 13 Tamil Nadu trust vote based on ‘vague’ allegations
content: The top court said the plea was based on “vague, wild and casual allegations” and therefore did not merit interference

title: Uproar in Kaushambi over vandalism of Ambedkar’s statue
content: The opposition said the incidents were part of a nefarious design by feudal forces, supported by the BJP, to suppress and humiliate the Dalit community

title: TVK, AIADMK, DMK and PMK claim credit for empowering SCs
content: Minister for Public Works Aadhav Arjuna appealed to parties to drop the term Thazhthapatta makkal (downtrodden people) and consider using a more neutral term — Scheduled Castes

title: Malabar River Festival: Kodenchery to host ‘Off-Road Fun Drive’
content: The event is being organised as part of the build-up to the 12th International White Water Kayaking Championship, a curtain-raiser to the Malabar River Festival

title: Kerala Revised Budget: In-principle approval for Cheemeni industrial park
content: The project, which is expected to generate around 15,000 employment opportunities, is among a series of development initiatives approved for the Trikaripur constituency

title: Drama at Kerala DHS office as two senior officials claim charge
content: Reena resumes office citing tribunal order, while Additional DHS retains charge pending official instructions

title: Kerala Budget: State set to make a transition from welfare-centric to knowledge-driven economy
content: The government plans to develop Kerala into a Knowledge Valley, making the State a preferred destination for higher education, including through special legislation to attract leading foreign universities and collaborations with premier institutions. For the start-up ecosystem, this is particularly important

title: Kerala Budget: New ideas weighed down by an old trilemma
content: The Kerala Budget lays much emphasis on new developmental themes, but lacks a clear road map for their implementation. Lower growth, weak tax buoyancy, and ambitious welfare commitments constitute a trilemma for the State

title: Kerala Revised Budget: Kasaragod district gets special focus
content: The Budget announces major measures in healthcare, education, infrastructure and endosulfan victim welfare

title: Kerala Revised Budget: ₹50 crore earmarked for establishing Tribal University in Wayanad
content: The proposed Tribal University and Indigenous Knowledge Zone is envisaged as a centre for higher learning, research, innovation, and entrepreneurship among tribal communities

title: Kerala Revised Budget: Malabar to get international football stadium as Budget boosts sports sector
content: The proposed stadium will feature modern facilities and is being planned in recognition of football’s enduring popularity in the region

title: Kerala Revised Budget: Cultural park in memory of MT gets ₹50 crore
content: The proposed park is expected to come up as a multidisciplinary cultural space featuring venues for various art forms

title: Supreme Court seized of granting leave to convicts pending appeal, amicus curiae informs five-judge Larger Bench of Madras High Court
content: Senior Counsel Abudu Kumar Rajaratnam informs the Larger Bench that the matter was listed last before the Supreme Court in April 2024, and is still pending adjudication

title: Kerala Revised Budget: New cancer centre on cards in Malappuram
content: Budget allocates ₹5 crore as an initial allocation for upgrading the Government Taluk Hospital in Malappuram town into a super-speciality general hospital

title: Kerala Revised Budget: MLAs welcome Budget allocations for Kannur, Irikkur constituencies
content: The MLAs say allocations will strengthen infrastructure, public services and connectivity while addressing several long-pending development demands in the two constituencies

title: AG flags irregularities in Gruha Lakshmi scheme implementation
content: One of the key findings relates to 19,020 beneficiaries who were found to be sharing bank account numbers;  3.03 lakh successful payments amounting to ₹60.69 crore were made to such beneficiaries

title: Kerala Revised Budget: Kozhikode Light Metro project gets fresh impetus
content: ₹20 crore allocated for project activities, feasibility study to be expedited, says CM; Calicut International Airport included in proposed aviation-logistics hub project

title: Health Minister calls for stronger action against substandard medicines in Andhra Pradesh
content: Satya Kumar Yadav directs officials to strengthen surveillance mechanisms and warns that negligence in safeguarding public health will not be tolerated

title: Bihar Police searches houses of two IAS officers over tender scam
content: Both officers are facing departmental proceedings and currently suspended for their involvement in the alleged tender scam

title: Parliamentary panel seeks clarity from government. on future engagement with Pakistan
content: Government says it desires “normal neighbourly relations” but emphasises that this has to “happen in an atmosphere free of violence and terrorist activity”

title: 'Ready to resign': Uddhav's emotional offer amid MPs rebellion, but with a condition
content: Uddhav Thackeray projected confidence amid a fresh party rebellion, assuring Shiv Sainiks are "fired up" despite six Lok Sabha MPs defecting. He apologized to voters for electing lawmakers who switched sides. Thackeray dismissed merger talks with Congress and defended his leadership, highlighting electoral wins. He warned of a threat to democracy with a move towards a "one party, no election" system. This latest crisis follows the 2022 split.

title: A tale of two cross-votings: How power politics played out in Karnataka, Jharkhand
content: Cross-voting significantly impacted Rajya Sabha elections in Jharkhandand Legislative Council elections in Karnataka, with both the INDIA bloc and NDA facing losses. While parties lauded rival cross-voters as exercising conscience, they condemned their own MLAs for betraying the party. This highlights a persistent double standard, where power dynamics, not ideology, drive these electoral outcomes despite past reforms aimed at curbing corruption.

title: California lawmakers pass UK and Canada-like software tax that US 'fought' to remove
content: California is set to implement a new sales tax on digital software downloads, impacting platforms like Slack and Google Workspace starting next year. Governor Newsom expects the measure, projected to generate $900 million annually, to primarily affect businesses. This move draws parallels to international digital services taxes, though California's approach expands existing sales tax to digital purchases for fairness, aiming to equalize tax burdens between physical and online software buyers.

title: Indian captain of sanctioned Russian tanker charged in UK; family seeks safe return
content: An Indian captain, Ajay Pant, has been arrested in the UK and charged with breaching sanctions after his Russian oil tanker was intercepted in the English Channel. The vessel, en route to India from Russia, was found to be stateless. Pant's family in Uttarakhand has appealed to the Indian government for his safe return, while authorities confirm consular access has been granted.

title: Pak man who crossed LoC sent back, handshakes mark handover
content: A Pakistani national, Asad Khan, apprehended last week after crossing the Line of Control in Kupwara, Jammu and Kashmir, has been safely repatriated. Khan, from Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, was detained near Samari village on June 12. The Indian Army stated he was treated with dignity and compassion, with his handover at the Aman Setu peace bridge highlighting professional military conduct and humanitarian values.

title: One of world’s richest persons & Louis Vuitton CEO on Elon Musk becoming trillionaire
content: Elon Musk has become the world's first trillionaire following SpaceX's historic public listing, with the company's valuation exceeding $2 trillion. Bernard Arnault, CEO of LVMH, acknowledged Musk's dominance in the rocket industry but clarified that the wealth represents company value, not liquid assets. Musk emphasized SpaceX's ambition to make humanity multi-planetary and space travel accessible to all.

title: Europe’s Russia dilemma: Why EU is opening a channel with Moscow despite Ukraine war
content: European Council President Antonio Costa defended his office's direct diplomatic outreach to Russia, emphasizing the need for communication channels despite the absence of immediate peace talks over Ukraine. While some EU leaders expressed concerns about uncoordinated efforts and maintaining pressure on Moscow, others, like Spain and Ireland, backed the initiative as a necessary diplomatic tool to convey the bloc's messages directly to Russia.

title: IPL 2027 Team Moves: Kuldeep Yadav set for LSG; Pant likely to return to DC
content: Kuldeep Yadav is set to join the Lucknow Super Giants for the 2027 IPL season. Meanwhile, Rishabh Pant is strongly expected to make a return to his former team, the Delhi Capitals, with the specifics of his move still being finalised. Both players had a torrid campaign in IPL 2026 for their respective teams.

title: Jeff Bezos’ ex-wife donates most of wealth to HBCUs after selling Amazon stake
content: MacKenzie Scott, a prominent philanthropist, has donated over $26 billion to 2,700 organizations, including significant contributions to Historically Black Colleges and Universities. Her trust-based approach involves unrestricted donations, empowering recipients to use funds freely. Despite her immense generosity, Scott's net worth remains substantial, and she remains committed to giving away her fortune.

title: The Mutual Fund retreat: When war panic meets your SIP - what investors should do now
content: Experts warn against panic selling, emphasizing the need to maintain investment discipline. In fact, the simplest advice is this: do nothing. That is the whole answer, and it is harder to follow than it sounds, he says.

title: Mob mistakes couple for child kidnappers, assaults them; 16 arrested
content: Two young development workers, on a fellowship field visit in Odisha's Rayagada district, were brutally attacked by a mob who mistook them for child kidnappers. Despite showing identity cards and explaining their purpose, the duo was chased, assaulted, and the woman was half-stripped. Police have arrested 16 individuals and are searching for more accused, registering four cases related to the terrifying ordeal.

title: Ronaldo fans face backlash for targeting João Neves' girlfriend online
content: Portugal midfielder Joao Neves has found himself at the center of a major social media controversy following Portugal’s 1-1 draw against DR Congo in their opening FIFA World Cup match. The young midfielder scored Portugal’s only goal, but much of the post-match discussion shifted toward Cristiano Ronaldo’s performance. The veteran forward struggled to make an impact during the game, leading to criticism from football fans.

title: Toronto Strengthens Defense with Blockbuster $68M Lightning Acquisition
content: John Chayka did not ease into the Toronto Maple Leafs job. His first major swing brought Darren Raddysh from Tampa Bay in a reported $68 million sign-and-trade, giving Toronto a new power-play weapon and a clear defensive reset after a rough season with pressure attached to the price and term. Chayka now owns the bet. So does Toronto’s blue line.

title: Taylor Swift returns to studio ahead of Travis Kelce wedding surprise
content: Taylor Swift’s latest Electric Lady Studios visit has fueled fresh speculation about a private wedding surprise for Travis Kelce. Reports from Daily Mail, TMZ, Page Six, and HT point to a guarded celebration, rumored venue shifts, NDA talk, and one alleged gift that could turn their wedding into a pop-culture event. Nothing is confirmed yet by the couple.

title: 21-year-old NEET aspirant dies after falling from 3rd floor in Indore
content: A 21-year-old medical aspirant in Indore, Avantika Maurya, tragically died after falling from a multi-storey building, two days before the NEET-UG re-test. Police are investigating the incident, which occurred while she was on a phone call. Her father mentioned she had been suffering from depression for five months. The incident has sparked political reactions, with the Congress blaming the ruling BJP over NEET irregularities.

title: 'Like closing a road because ... ': CJP founder's dig at Centre after Telegram ban
content: Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke slammed the Centre's Telegram ban ahead of the NEET re-exam, calling it a superficial fix. He highlighted ongoing student suicides and criticized the education system's commercialization. Dipke urged political leaders to prioritize student welfare over political gains, as Delhi Police bolstered security for an upcoming CJP protest.

title: Bihar raises circle rates: Here's what it means for property buyers and sellers
content: Bihar's property registration rates have significantly increased, with rural land rates jumping 60% and urban rates doubling. An annual 5% MVR hike and a 1% stamp duty increase are now in effect. This move aims to boost farmer compensation for land acquisition, which will see substantial hikes for both state and central projects. Women buyers will also benefit from an increased stamp duty concession.

title: 'Neither I nor Italy ever beg': Meloni hits back at Trump after his photo remark
content: Giorgia Meloni, the Italian Prime Minister, firmly dismissed Donald Trump's allegations that she asked him for a photo at the G7 summit, calling his statements 'entirely fictitious.' Expressing her surprise and disappointment, Meloni stated that neither she nor her country would ever resort to begging. This confrontation signifies a potential rift in the previously friendly relationship between Meloni and Trump.

title: 'Just a trailer': Eknath Shinde warns as Uddhav Sena faces revolt 2.0
content: Shiv Sena (UBT)'s Lok Sabha unit appeared on the verge of a formal split on Thursday after six of its nine MPs stayed away from a parliamentary party meeting in Delhi despite the party issuing a whip. Only Arvind Sawant, Anil Desai and Rajabhau Waze attended the meeting, reaffirming their loyalty to party chief Uddhav Thackeray.

title: India XI for Chennai ODI: Jaiswal set to open with Rohit, Nitish Reddy returns
content: Team India's assistant coach has made it clear that India's top three -- Yashasvi Jaiswal, Rohit Sharma and captain Shubman Gill will remain intact. Left-arm seamer Arshdeep Singh is likely to be rested. Between Ishan Kishan, Shreyas Iyer and KL Rahul one will miss out as management wants all-rounders Washington Sundar and Nitish Kumar Reddy to get a fair run.

title: OFFSIDE | World Cup 2026: Canadian joy, Brazilian anxiety, and Nike's unofficial win
content: Across the globe, the stereotype of Canada, when seen through an American lens, is that it’s a country inhabited by extremely polite people with the exception of Wolverine. But Canadians, when push comes to shove, can put up a mean face, as Mark Carney showed Donald Trump.

title: Why Ronaldo's FIFA World Cup opener wasn't as simple as zero goals
content: Cristiano Ronaldo's blank outing against DR Congo reignited questions over his place in Portugal's World Cup side. But the numbers paint a more complicated picture. While the 41-year-old failed to register a shot on target, FIFA tracking data shows he made 47 off-ball runs and was found only 10 times. His finishing deserves scrutiny, but Portugal's lack of service and creativity may have been just as responsible for a frustrating opening night.

title: Happy Father's Day 2026: Top 50 wishes, messages and quotes for your dad
content: Fathers often express love through actions, not words, and Father's Day on June 21, 2026, is a time to appreciate their guidance and support. Simple gestures like cooking a favorite meal or sharing memories can mean more than gifts. This day is about acknowledging their sacrifices and letting them know they are deeply appreciated.

title: Sitharaman says external aid projects should help Northeast products reach global markets
content: Union finance minister Nirmala Sitharaman emphasized that externally aided projects in the Northeast should empower local products for global markets and strengthen livelihoods. She highlighted a seven-fold increase in assistance, with over 10,000 km of roads built since 2014. Sitharaman underscored the region's potential, stating that EAPs offer a development model combining central support, state execution, and global expertise for local communities.

title: How AI could transform your future home
content: Generative AI is poised to revolutionize India's real estate, potentially adding $14-17 billion over seven years by boosting efficiency and decision-making. This technology promises faster project launches, accelerated sales, and cost reductions. Beyond construction, AI will enhance the home-buying experience through personalized recommendations and better understanding of buyer needs, leading to more responsive housing solutions.

title: 'If not for Samson, would India have won?': Sreesanth takes aim at Gambhir
content: Former pacer S Sreesanth has questioned head coach Gautam Gambhir's significant contribution to India's recent white-ball triumphs, emphasizing the crucial roles played by players like Sanju Samson and Suryakumar Yadav. Sreesanth argued that on-field leaders and players made key decisions, contrasting this with a more involved coach like Ashish Nehra. He also linked India's Test struggles to Gambhir's tenure, advocating for a mentor over a coach.

title: NTA to conduct nationwide mock drill on June 20 ahead of NEET-UG re-examination
content: Ahead of the NEET-UG 2026 re-examination on June 21, the National Testing Agency (NTA) will conduct a nationwide mock drill on June 20. This measure aims to ensure a seamless process for the nearly 23 lakh candidates. The NTA is actively communicating with students, urging them to download fresh admit cards and warning against fraudulent messages.

title: India's world-class airports and the first-impression challenge facing its cities
content: Many Indian cities struggle to impress travellers from the air, often revealing informal settlements and pollution, unlike the orderly, greener landscapes of Singapore, Bangkok, or Colombo. This stark contrast stems from rapid urbanisation, immense population density, and governance challenges, despite India's economic growth. While infrastructure is improving, the sheer scale of the problem presents a significant hurdle.

title: Anne Hathaway expecting third baby with husband Adam Shulman - watch announcement video
content: Anne Hathaway is expecting her third child with husband Adam Shulman, joyfully announcing the news on Instagram with a heartwarming video. The actress, already mother to sons Jonathan and Jack, shared her delight in this phase of family life, describing it as a "wonderful time." Hathaway previously spoke about how motherhood profoundly transformed her perspective, making her feel more grounded and true to herself as she embraces this new chapter.

title: US-Iran war impact: India’s Russian oil imports may touch all-time high in June
content: With a US sanctions waiver in place for three months, India stepped up Russian crude oil procurement, and import levels touched near all-time highs. According to analysis of data by Sumit Ritolia, Manager Modelling and Refining at Kpler, India’s crude imports from Russia could hit an all time high of 2.35 million barrels per day in June.

title: RIL AGM: From Jio Platforms IPO & satellite broadband focus to AI plans - top takeaways
content: While the proposed Jio IPO drew significant attention, Ambani used the company’s annual shareholder meeting to lay out Reliance’s future growth strategy, identifying artificial intelligence as one of the most important pillars of the group’s next chapter.

title: Israel, Hezbollah agree to ceasefire after IDF strikes kill 19 in Lebanon
content: A ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah is scheduled to commence on Friday afternoon, marking a significant pause in months of hostilities along the border. With mediation efforts from Qatar, the US, and Iran, this agreement seeks to ease tensions and avert a larger conflict in the region. Although neither party has commented publicly, there is cautious optimism for peace and stability in areas affected by the turmoil.

title: Ex-DRDO scientist accused of sharing secrets with Pakistani agent to face trial
content: A former DRDO scientist, Pradeep Kurulkar, faces charges in an espionage case, with trial set to begin next month. Accused of sharing classified defence information with a suspected Pakistani intelligence agent, Kurulkar's plea for discharge was rejected. Proceedings will be held in-camera due to the sensitive nature of the Official Secrets Act violations.

title: The first woman TTE on Darjeeling’s iconic toy train who became a tourist favourite
content: Sarita Yolmo, a lifelong admirer of the Darjeeling Himalayan Railway, has become its first female Train Ticket Examiner. Her childhood fascination with the toy train transformed into a career spanning over three decades. Now, at 55, she navigates the UNESCO World Heritage route, a testament to dedication and a fulfilling journey that began with a simple childhood dream.

title: 14-year-old sold for Rs 32,000 under pretext of marriage, gangraped; 4 arrested
content: Authorities in Ajmer, Rajasthan, have apprehended four individuals in connection with the trafficking and gang-rape of a 14-year-old girl. The minor was allegedly sold for Rs 32,000 under the guise of marriage. Rescued from Churu district, the girl's statement and medical evidence led to charges under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita and POCSO Act. Investigations are ongoing to locate a fifth suspect.

title: 'Hope Speaker gives us justice': Abhishek files disqualification pleas against rebel MPs
content: Expressing hope that Lok Sabha Om Birla​​​​​​ would "act in accordance with the Constitution," Banerjee said: "The Lok Sabha Speaker is the custodian of the House, not the protector of the government of the day. Every citizen, especially elected representatives, must work within the ambit of the law. Therefore, one has to function according to the provisions laid down in the Constitution."

title: Exclusive: Yuvraj Singh to take IPL coaching plunge with Delhi Capitals
content: Former India cricketer Yuvraj Singh is set to join the Delhi Capitals support staff from the 2027 IPL season. This marks his first formal coaching role in the league, building on his extensive mentorship of young players. His hands-on approach and impact on careers has been considered to be highly valued.

title: Agartala-bound IndiGo flight hit by lightning at Kolkata airport
content: An IndiGo aircraft at Kolkata airport was struck by lightning on Friday during a severe thunderstorm. While no passengers were harmed, two ground personnel were briefly affected and received medical evaluation. The flight was later operated by a different aircraft.

title: Manipur ethnic violence: 10 accused arrested in joint operation
content: The detained individuals are accused of playing active roles in various incidents of violence, including attacks on security forces, looting of arms, and bank robberies

title: Supreme Court asks tribunal to speed up advocate’s appeal in SIR row
content: The Bench remarked that a mechanism comprising former High Court judges had already been put in place to adjudicate disputes arising out of deletions from the electoral rolls and said the petitioner ought to pursue his remedy before the concerned forum

title: Desertions, resignations and arrests of Trinamool Congress leaders continue in West Bengal
content: Jyoti Priya Mallick, former Minister in Trinamool government resigned from the party, Gautam Deb quit as mayor of Siliguri Municipal Corporation and former Trinamool MLA Ramendu Sinharoy was arrested by West Bengal Police in Belagavi

title: Watch: TN Assembly adopts resolution against Karnataka’s Mekedatu dam plan | Above the Fold | 19.06.2026
content: In this episode we look at the Tamil Nadu Assembly revives the Cauvery dispute with a resolution against Karnataka’s Mekedatu dam project, while Kerala’s first budget under Chief Minister V.D. Satheesan sparks political debate. We also look at Israel-Hezbollah clashes test a fragile U.S.-Iran deal, and more.

title: Four transgender persons arrested for robbing motorist near Sira on NH-48
content: The alleged incident took place in the early hours of June 14 when the complainant had stopped his car at a roadside rest area on the Tumakuru-Hiriyur stretch of NH-48 to take a break

title: Ban on Telegram stays; it’s justified, says Delhi HC
content: Govt. followed procedure as per Section 69A of IT Act and gave reasons for invoking emergency powers, says court; definition of “information” includes software and programmes, it says

title: PM Modi to visit West Bengal for Paschim Banga Dibas,  International Yoga Day celebrations
content: The event to mark West Bengal Day will take place in Tarakeswar, Hooghly district, a location steeped in heritage due to its connection with Dr. Syama Prasad Mookerjee and his efforts in preserving the culture of West Bengal

title: Future of Telangana is in the classrooms, says Revanth
content: Students are assets of the present and the future, says Governor Shukla, calling for united fight against drug abuse he administered an anti-drug pledge to the assembled students

title: Help hospitals in Gaza and West Bank, Palestine envoy urges India
content: Hospitals running out of cancer drugs and other life-saving medicines, dialysis filters, insulin, surgical supplies, says Abu Shawesh

title: SIR explained: What Telangana voters need to know
content: As Telangana gears up for a door-to-door verification of voters, the Special Intensive Revision has emerged as one of the most closely watched electoral exercises in recent years. While election officials describe it as a routine effort to improve the accuracy of voter lists, political parties and electors have raised questions about implementation and the treatment of anomalies in voter data

title: Implementation Of Guarantees To Change, Karnataka To Bring In New Checks
content: One of the key changes under consideration is stricter scrutiny of beneficiaries. The government has found instances where benefits continued to be credited to beneficiaries who have passed away.

title: Stealth Frigate 'Dunagiri', 2 Other Ships To Be Inducted Into Navy on Sunday
content: 'Dunagiri' is equipped with advanced weapons and sensors, including BrahMos surface-to-surface missiles and the Medium Range Surface-to-Air Missile system, significantly enhancing the Navy's combat capabilities.

title: India Gets Vice-Presidency Of Global Anti-Money Laundering Body FATF
content: Union Culture Secretary Vivek Aggarwal was appointed as the vice president of the body at the conclusion of the plenary meeting held at its headquarters in Paris, an official statement said.

title: Massive Anti-Terror Agency NIA's Crackdown Across Manipur, 10 Arrested
content: Officials said the operation was based on intelligence inputs and evidence gathered during ongoing investigations.

title: Eknath Shinde-Uddhav Thackeray Duel Features Tigers, Dogs And Sholay
content: This duelling comes against the backdrop of a fresh crisis for the Uddhav Thackeray-led Shiv Sena (UBT), with six of its nine Lok Sabha MPs expected to join the rival Shiv Sena led by Eknath Shinde.

title: Karnataka Plans Tighter Checks On Guarantee Schemes To Curb Misuse
content: The state is also expected to strengthen checks on UPI-linked and payment bank accounts following reports of misuse through certain digital banking platforms.

title: Fire Breaks Out Near Ayodhya Helipad Minutes Before Yogi Adityanath's Arrival
content: Police have launched an investigation to ascertain the cause of the fire and determine how it broke out close to the helipad designated for the chief minister's arrival.

title: Search For Missing Andhra Toddler Enters 10th Day, Cops Warn Against Rumours
content: Gnaneswari went missing on June 6 from a plantation at CH Agraharam village in Dondavaka Panchayat, where her father works as a caretaker.

title: "Genocidal Death Cult": Araghchi Attacks Israel After 47 Killed In Lebanon
content: Israel and Hezbollah agreed to renew their ceasefire, according to a US official, after fresh violence in Lebanon put the fragile arrangement under pressure.

title: Northeast Can Become Growth Driver Through Organic Farming: N Sitharaman
content: Sitharaman emphasised that states such as Meghalaya need not compete with larger agricultural producers on scale alone.

title: Israel-Hezbollah Reach Ceasefire, But No Clarity on Lebanon's Occupied Areas
content: Israel and Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah have reached a ceasefire agreement, a US official announced on Friday after an escalation in fighting in Lebanon threatened the chances of an interim agreement on ending the war in Iran.

title: "They're Finished. No Air Force, Navy, They Get No Money": Trump On Iran Deal
content: The deal, signed this week by President Donald Trump and his Iranian counterpart Masoud Pezeshkian, aims to end a conflict that began on February 28 with US-Israeli strikes on Iran.

title: In Bhagwant Mann Video Row, A Battle Of Competing Narratives
content: The controversy has entered a complicated phase because there are now two competing narratives backed by separate sets of forensic findings.

title: Sharad Pawar vs Indira Gandhi To Shiv Sena Now: Maharashtra's History Of Defections
content: The latest crisis engulfing the Shiv Sena is another chapter in a story that stretches back decades to the fragmentation of the Congress in the late 1960s and the rise of Sharad Pawar as a young rebel.

title: Strait Of Hormuz Traffic Highest In 2 Months, Thanks To US-Iran Deal
content: A total of 25 commercial vessels crossed the newly reopened strait on Thursday, the highest number since mid-April, according to data from tracking firm AXSMarine.

title: US Needs $80 Billion For Iran War, Other Bills As Trump Faces Heat: Report
content: President Donald Trump has faced backlash from Americans who accuse him of pouring billions of taxpayer dollars into the Middle East conflict while oil prices and inflation skyrocket in the United States.

title: Probe Agency ED Raids 6 Bengaluru Crypto Firms Over Rs 2,500-Crore Violations
content: Five companies allegedly used virtual digital assets to facilitate unauthorised cross-border money transfers; bank accounts with around Rs 6 crore frozen.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Reform Without Rupture: Narendra Modi Through A Burkean Conservative Lens
content: PM Modi comes from a distinctly Indic political and intellectual background, not the Anglo-European conservative tradition. Even so, a comparison can help highlight certain similarities in how both approach tradition, change and political authority.

title: Swiss Miss: Vance cans trip for Iran talks as critics go nuclear over US ditching Israel
content: the setback to the peace process came even as Trump is being pilloried by critics at home . the white house postponed vice president JD Vance’s planned visit to Switzerland to sign the moU and initiate talks with an Iranian team . he has long been on stand-by to fly out for a formal signing ceremony and an initial round of talks .

title: Owner of rehabilitation centre hacked to death by inmates
content: your active subscription(s) Account subscription benefits alongside Premium Stories, Editorials, Opinions and more .
